title_en: The Building of the Islamic State in Medina
content_en:
The Prophet's ﷺ arrival in Medina was immediately followed by a series of foundational acts that demonstrate a remarkable statesman's vision: in the span of a few months, he created institutions that would bind a fractured community together and establish the moral and structural foundations of what would become a state.

The first act was the construction of the Prophet's Mosque (al-Masjid al-Nabawi). The Prophet ﷺ allowed his camel (Qaswa) to kneel where it wished, and when she settled and knelt again at a date-palm grove, that land was purchased and cleared, and the mosque was built by the Prophet ﷺ himself alongside his Companions. The mosque was not merely a place of prayer but the civic heart of the new community — a court of law, a meeting hall, a school, a place of welfare for the poor, and the home of the Prophet ﷺ himself, whose private chambers were built adjoining the mosque's eastern wall.

The second foundational act was the institution of brotherhood (al-muakhah) between the Muhajirin (emigrants from Mecca) and the Ansar (helpers of Medina). The Prophet ﷺ personally paired each emigrant with a Medinan helper, creating legal bonds of brotherhood that were so tight they initially included mutual inheritance rights. The generosity of the Ansar in this process became legendary — some offered to share their homes, their date orchards, and even proposed divorcing one of their wives so that their new brother might marry her. The Prophet ﷺ praised the Ansar with words he never took back: 'Were it not for the Hijra, I would have been one of the Ansar.'

The third foundational act was the Constitution of Medina (Sahifat al-Madinah or Wathiqat al-Madinah) — a written document between the Prophet ﷺ, the various tribes of Medina, and the Jewish communities of the city. This document, considered one of the earliest written constitutions in human history, established the relationships and obligations between the various groups under the umbrella of the new polity. It recognized the rights and obligations of all parties, established the Prophet ﷺ as the final arbiter of disputes, and declared that all signatories would collectively defend Medina against external aggression.

Parallel to these administrative achievements, the prayer direction (qiblah) was changed from Jerusalem to Mecca in the second year of Hijra — a moment of immense theological and symbolic significance — and the call to prayer (adhan) was instituted. The Friday congregational prayer (Jumu'ah) was established, the zakah (obligatory charity) was systematized, and the Ramadan fast was prescribed. The first Islamic state was thus not only a political entity but a moral community governed by divine law, a vision of society utterly unlike anything that had previously existed in Arabia.
content_id:
Kedatangan Nabi ﷺ di Madinah segera diikuti oleh serangkaian tindakan-tindakan fondasi yang menunjukkan visi seorang negarawan yang luar biasa: dalam jangka waktu beberapa bulan, beliau menciptakan institusi-institusi yang akan menyatukan komunitas yang terpecah dan meletakkan fondasi moral dan struktural dari apa yang akan menjadi sebuah negara.

Tindakan pertama adalah membangun masjid di lokasi tempat unta beliau, Qaswa', berlutut — yang kemudian menjadi Masjid Nabawi, pusat kehidupan komunitas Islam. Masjid ini berfungsi sebagai tempat ibadah, pengadilan, pusat pendidikan, dan pusat komando militer sekaligus.

Tindakan kedua adalah mempersaudarakan kaum Muhajirin (Muslim yang hijrah dari Makkah) dengan kaum Anshar (Muslim Madinah yang menyambut mereka). Setiap Muhajirin dipasangkan dengan seorang Anshar sebagai saudara dalam agama, yang berbagi harta dan rumah. Ini adalah tindakan solidaritas sosial yang tidak tertandingi dalam sejarah manusia.

Tindakan ketiga adalah Piagam Madinah (Shahifah al-Madinah) — perjanjian tertulis yang mengatur hubungan antara kaum Muslim berbagai suku, kaum Yahudi Madinah, dan kelompok-kelompok lainnya. Piagam ini menetapkan hak-hak, kewajiban-kewajiban, dan mekanisme penyelesaian sengketa. Para sarjana modern menganggapnya sebagai salah satu dokumen konstitusional pertama dalam sejarah manusia.

Fondasi-fondasi ini — masjid, persaudaraan, dan pakta sosial — adalah tiga pilar yang di atasnya Nabi ﷺ membangun komunitas Islam di Madinah.
